translation: In civil enforcement proceedings, it is not uncommon for an outsider to mistakenly transfer funds into the account of the judgment debtor due to operational errors. The focus of disputes in such cases is: whether the mistakenly transferred funds constitute the judgment debtor's liable property? Can the outsider exclude enforcement through the enforcement objection procedure? This article, based on an enforcement case in which our firm's lawyers successfully recovered funds, analyzes the legal rules and practical points of such cases.
